News Application Modules¶

news.models¶

Models for the News Application.

class news.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

News article model with editorial workflow.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

STATUS_CHOICES = [('draft', 'Draft'), ('pending', 'Pending Review'), ('published', 'Published'), ('rejected', 'Rejected')]¶
approve(reviewer)[source]¶
author¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

author_id¶
category¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

category_id¶
clean()[source]¶

Hook for doing any extra model-wide validation after clean() has been called on every field by self.clean_fields. Any ValidationError raised by this method will not be associated with a particular field; it will have a special-case association with the field defined by NON_FIELD_ERRORS.

comments¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

content¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

created_at¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

excerpt¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

featured_image¶

Just like the FileDescriptor, but for ImageFields. The only difference is assigning the width/height to the width_field/height_field, if appropriate.

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

save(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Save the current instance. Override this in a subclass if you want to control the saving process.

The ‘force_insert’ and ‘force_update’ parameters can be used to insist that the “save” must be an SQL insert or update (or equivalent for non-SQL backends), respectively. Normally, they should not be set.

class news.models.UserProfile(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

Extended user profile with role-based access control.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

ROLE_CHOICES = [('reader', 'Reader'), ('journalist', 'Journalist'), ('editor', 'Editor')]¶
ROLE_EDITOR = 'editor'¶
ROLE_JOURNALIST = 'journalist'¶
ROLE_READER = 'reader'¶
avatar¶

Just like the FileDescriptor, but for ImageFields. The only difference is assigning the width/height to the width_field/height_field, if appropriate.

bio¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

created_at¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

user¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a one-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Restaurant(Model):
    place = OneToOneField(Place, related_name='restaurant')

Restaurant.place is a ForwardOneToOneDescriptor instance.

user_id¶

news.views¶

Views for the News Application.

news.views.approve_article(request, pk)[source]¶

Approve an article for publication.

news.views.article_detail(request, pk)[source]¶

Display a single published article and its comments.

news.views.article_list(request)[source]¶

Display a list of articles with filtering options.

news.views.article_preview(request, pk)[source]¶

Allow authors and editors to preview unpublished articles.

news.views.create_article(request)[source]¶

Handle article creation.

news.views.create_publisher(request)[source]¶

Allow editors to create publishers.

news.views.dashboard(request)[source]¶

Redirect to the appropriate dashboard based on the user’s role.

news.views.delete_article(request, pk)[source]¶

Allow editors to delete an article.

news.views.edit_article(request, pk)[source]¶

Handle article editing.

news.views.editor_dashboard(request)[source]¶

Display the editor dashboard.

news.views.forgot_password(request)[source]¶

Handle password reset request.

news.views.home(request)[source]¶

Display the home page with recent published articles.

news.views.journalist_dashboard(request)[source]¶

Display the journalist dashboard.

news.views.journalist_detail(request, username)[source]¶

Display a journalist profile and published articles.

news.views.publisher_detail(request, slug)[source]¶

Display a single publisher’s page.

news.views.publisher_list(request)[source]¶

Display list of all publishers.

news.views.reader_dashboard(request)[source]¶

Display the reader dashboard.

news.views.register(request)[source]¶

User registration view.

news.views.reject_article(request, pk)[source]¶

Reject an article.

news.views.reset_password(request, token)[source]¶

Handle password reset with token.

news.views.submit_article(request, pk)[source]¶

Submit an article for review.

news.views.subscribe_journalist(request, username)[source]¶

Subscribe to a journalist.

news.views.subscribe_publisher(request, slug)[source]¶

Subscribe to a publisher.

news.views.subscriptions(request)[source]¶

Display a reader’s subscriptions.

news.views.unsubscribe_journalist(request, username)[source]¶

Unsubscribe from a journalist.

news.views.unsubscribe_publisher(request, slug)[source]¶

Unsubscribe from a publisher.

news.views.user_login(request)[source]¶

User login view.

news.views.user_logout(request)[source]¶

Handle user logout.

news.api_views¶

API Views for the News Application.

news.api_views.article_detail_api(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

API endpoint to retrieve a single article.

news.api_views.article_list_api(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

API endpoint to list all published articles.

news.api_views.category_list_api(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

API endpoint to list all categories.

news.api_views.comment_create_api(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

API endpoint to create a comment.

news.api_views.publisher_list_api(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

API endpoint to list all publishers.

news.api_views.subscription_list_api(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

API endpoint to list user’s subscriptions.

news.serializers¶

Serializers for the News Application API.

class news.serializers.ArticleDetailSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serialize a full article payload including related objects.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'title', 'slug', 'content', 'excerpt', 'author', 'category', 'publisher', 'status', 'created_at', 'published_at']¶
model¶

alias of Article

class news.serializers.ArticleListSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serialize article fields required for collection/list endpoints.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'title', 'slug', 'excerpt', 'author_name', 'category_name', 'status', 'created_at']¶
model¶

alias of Article

class news.serializers.CategorySerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serialize article category metadata.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'name', 'slug']¶
model¶

alias of Category

class news.serializers.CommentSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Validate and serialize reader comments for article APIs.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'article', 'author_name', 'content', 'created_at']¶
model¶

alias of Comment

read_only_fields = ['id', 'created_at']¶
validate_article(value)[source]¶

Ensure comments are attached only to published articles.

class news.serializers.PublisherSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serialize publisher details for list and detail API responses.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'name', 'slug', 'description', 'website']¶
model¶

alias of Publisher

class news.serializers.SubscriptionSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serialize subscriptions with computed target information.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'target_type', 'target_name', 'subscribed_at']¶
model¶

alias of Subscription

read_only_fields = ['id', 'subscribed_at']¶
class news.serializers.UserSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serialize basic public fields for Django users.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'username', 'email']¶
model¶

alias of User

news.forms¶

Forms for the News Application.

class news.forms.ArticleForm(*args, allowed_publishers=None, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelForm

Form for creating and editing articles.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

fields = ['title', 'slug', 'content', 'excerpt', 'featured_image', 'publisher', 'category']¶
model¶

alias of Article

news.signals¶

Django signals for the News Application.

news.signals.create_user_profile(sender, instance, created, **kwargs)[source]¶

Create a UserProfile automatically when a new User is created.

news.signals.notify_subscribers_on_publication(sender, instance, **kwargs)[source]¶

Create notifications when a subscribed article is published.

news.signals.save_user_profile(sender, instance, **kwargs)[source]¶

Save the UserProfile when the User is saved.
